Libraries

Loading…

Upload & Jobs

A .zip/.tar/.tar.gz/.tar.xz/.7z/.rar of raw page images (one subfolder per volume, or images directly for a single volume), or a .pdf. Text-layer PDFs convert straight to EPUB with no OCR; scanned PDFs and archives are queued for GPU OCR. The resulting EPUBs appear in the library once done. A standalone .html file is copied straight into the library as-is — no OCR, no queue, appears within seconds. A .epub is inspected automatically: if it already has real, selectable text it's added as-is, no OCR; if it's just page images with no text layer, its pages are queued for GPU OCR like any other scan.

Library
Name
Book

Manage Books

Reconvert a book — or just one of its volumes — with a full OCR re-run, useful after a converter/mokuro update. This redoes OCR from the original raw scan (not a rebuild from cached results), so it takes as long as the first conversion did. Reconvert only works while the book's original raw scan is still staged on disk ("no raw scan" otherwise) — "Download images" always works instead, pulling the page images back out of the already-built EPUB, e.g. to test the upload flow without re-sourcing the original scan. Each book is collapsed to one row by default — click its name to see individual volumes — and shows when it was last analyzed; use the search box to filter a large library by name.
